4 · Page names for Google and browser tabs

Done

Every page now has a proper name for browser tabs, Google results, and link previews - “Our Team | Kidsister” and so on. Content In Action had none, so Google was writing its own. Also fixed: a leftover setting that would have hidden the new Garlic Girl page from Google.

5 · Garlic Girl Vintage case study

Done

The full case study, built from your flow doc and laid out by the same categories as your asset folder: Logo & Brand Identity (logo, sidewalk sign, three logo variations, the full color palette), Branded Apparel Design (tote + matchboxes), Store Signage & Assets (the lamp and the sticker, side by side), a sparkle divider band before the testimonial, and Vanessa’s testimonial. It gets its own page at /garlic-girl and a card on the Case Studies page. We also fixed two bugs on this page: a header that showed the wrong client’s name, and some empty space that showed up on large monitors.

6 · Content In Action

Done

Everything from your Scroll Content folder is now on the Content In Action page: the six vertical videos and the Team Taco photo set. The videos sit in the feed as tall, phone-style players that match how your current ones look, and play when you tap them. The Team Taco photos are one swipeable carousel, all fourteen of them, with arrows on desktop and swipe on phones.

Your page already breaks up the grid with little doodle stickers every few rows. We kept that going through the new content with a rainbow, a peace sign, and one new addition to the family, a red starburst, so the new rows feel like part of the same page instead of tacked on the end.

The whole page, top to bottom, before and after.

Revised July 7 The new videos and the Team Taco carousel now lead the page, right at the top of the grid, newest first like you asked.

Getting found on Google - quick wins we spotted

For a future round - nothing here is in this release

The goal: when someone in Springfield searches “marketing agency” or “branding agency,” Kidsister should be one of the names they see. Those searches currently show several other local agencies, and Kidsister isn’t in the results yet.

This round fixed the basics: every page has a real name Google can read, and new case study pages are no longer skipped. That doesn’t move rankings by itself.

The next three, in payoff order:

  • Google Business Profile. Free listing that puts you in the map box and shows your reviews. The biggest single win.
  • Say “Springfield” where it counts. The site barely uses the words people type into Google.
  • Make the contact page load fast. Slow pages rank lower and lose people.

Can scope this as its own round if you want it.

Contact form: making the wait feel right

Done

You’d mentioned the form sometimes looked like it was missing fields. We tracked that down: the form loads from JotForm’s servers, and for a second or two while it’s arriving that space can look blank or half-drawn. It sorts itself out on its own, and it works the vast majority of the time, but that first moment read as broken.

So there’s now a small loading animation that fills the space while the form comes in, in your ink color, and disappears the moment the form is ready. Same form, same email, same Slack. Nothing about how it works changed, the wait just looks intentional now instead of empty.

If you ever wanted it fully instant, the fix would be rebuilding the form natively in Framer and quietly handing submissions to JotForm behind the scenes, so your email and Slack alerts keep working exactly as they do now. Honestly though, I don’t think that’s worth it for you right now. It works nearly every time as is, and the loading animation covers the gap.
